steps.title: Turn the g table into an f table
steps.content:
The table gives $g(x)$, but the question asks for the y-intercept of $y=f(x)$. Since $g(x)=\dfrac{f(x)}{x+4}$, multiply each table output by $x+4$ to get $f(x)$:
  • $f(-24)=3(-24+4)=-60$
  • $f(-9)=0(-9+4)=0$
  • $f(16)=5(16+4)=100$

steps.title: Find the linear function f
steps.content: Use two of the converted points, $(-9,0)$ and $(16,100)$. The slope is $$m=\frac{100-0}{16-(-9)}=\frac{100}{25}=4.$$ Since $f(-9)=0$, use $f(x)=4x+b$:$$0=4(-9)+b\Rightarrow b=36$$So $f(x)=4x+36$.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is where $x=0$. From $f(x)=4x+36$, the intercept is $(0,36)$. That confirms A is correct.
